title: Gyeran Mari Korean Rolled Omelette

seo.title: Easy Korean Rolled Omelette (Gyeran Mari) Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of Gyeran Mari, the delightful Korean Rolled Omelette. Our easy, authentic recipe guides you step-by-step to a perfect breakfast or snack. Try it today!
longTailKeywords: best Korean Rolled Omelette at home
seo.h1: Gyeran Mari: Authentic Korean Rolled Omelette Recipe
h2Headings: What is Gyeran Mari (Korean Rolled Omelette)?
Ingredients You'll Need for Gyeran Mari
Step-by-Step Guide to Making Korean Rolled Omelette
Pro Tips for the Best Gyeran Mari
Serving Suggestions for Your Rolled Omelette

seo.imageAlt: A perfectly rolled Gyeran Mari, a Korean Rolled Omelette, sliced and ready to serve on a white plate with chopsticks.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy this beautifully layered Gyeran Mari, a traditional Korean Rolled Omelette, perfect for breakfast, lunch, or a snack!

description: Gyeran Mari is a delightful Korean rolled omelette, known for its beautiful golden layers and vibrant vegetable fillings. This savory dish is a staple in Korean households, perfect for a nutritious breakfast, a light lunch, or a charming side dish in a bento box. Its soft, fluffy texture and subtle flavors make it a beloved comfort food for all ages.

instructions.title: Prepare the Egg Mixture
instructions.text: In a large bowl, crack the eggs. Add salt, sugar (if using), and water or milk. Whisk thoroughly until the yolks and whites are fully combined and slightly frothy. Do not over-whisk, as too much air can cause bubbles. Strain the egg mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into another bowl to remove any chalazae or shell fragments, ensuring a smooth omelette.

instructions.title: Add Vegetables
instructions.text: Stir in the finely diced carrots and chopped green onions into the strained egg mixture. Ensure the vegetables are evenly distributed throughout the mixture. If desired, you can also add other finely minced vegetables like bell peppers or zucchini.

instructions.title: Heat the Pan
instructions.text: Heat a non-stick rectangular or round pan (about 8-10 inches) over medium-low heat. Add a very thin layer of cooking oil and spread it evenly with a paper towel. It's crucial to maintain a consistent low heat to prevent the omelette from browning too quickly.

instructions.title: Pour First Layer
instructions.text: Pour about 1/4 to 1/3 of the egg mixture into the hot pan, tilting the pan to spread it into a thin, even layer. Let it cook until the edges are set and the top is mostly set but still slightly wet. This usually takes about 1-2 minutes.

instructions.title: Roll the Omelette
instructions.text: Once the first layer is mostly set, gently roll the omelette from one end to about halfway or two-thirds across the pan using a spatula. Push the rolled portion to one side of the pan, leaving an empty space. Add a tiny bit more oil to the empty space if needed.

instructions.title: Add Subsequent Layers and Finish Rolling
instructions.text: Pour another 1/4 to 1/3 of the egg mixture into the empty space and slightly under the rolled portion. Lift the rolled omelette to allow the new egg mixture to flow underneath. Let it cook until mostly set, then continue rolling the existing omelette over the new layer. Repeat this process until all the egg mixture is used, creating multiple layers. Once fully rolled, cook for an additional minute on each side to ensure it's cooked through and lightly golden.

instructions.title: Slice and Serve
instructions.text: Carefully transfer the finished Gyeran Mari onto a cutting board. Let it cool for a few minutes to firm up, which makes slicing easier. Using a sharp knife, slice the rolled omelette into 1-inch thick pieces. Serve warm with rice, kimchi, or as a side dish.

tips: Strain the egg mixture for a smoother, more uniform texture without air bubbles.
Keep the heat consistently low to prevent browning and allow for gentle rolling. A rectangular pan makes rolling easier.
Let the Gyeran Mari cool for a few minutes before slicing; this helps it firm up and prevents it from falling apart.
article: Gyeran Mari Korean Rolled Omelette is a beloved Korean Breakfast Recipes dish enjoyed across Korean. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
